channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alleged rhino poaching kingpin Joseph ‘Big Joe’ Nyalungu shot dead in Mpumalanga
item.description: Joseph Nyalungu, better known as ‘Big Joe’, a former police officer from Hazyview and alleged kingpin in Mpumalanga’s rhino poaching syndicates, was shot dead near his business premises in Mkhuhlu on ...

item.title: JMFyang and KaiKyle Perform Memorable Duets on 'ASAP XP'
item.description: On May 17, 2026, ABS-CBN's popular Sunday musical program 'ASAP XP' featured performances by the dynamic duo JMFyang and KaiKyle. The two young artists captivated audiences with their harmonious duets...

item.title: Philippine authorities investigate steel plant for hazardous materials
item.description: CAGAYAN DE ORO CITY, Philippines — Government authorities conducted a raid at the Philippine Sanjia Steel Corp. in Tagoloan, Misamis Oriental after reports indicated the presence of hazardous material...

item.title: Multiple Injuries Reported After Climax Coach Bus Accident at Chepsir, Kericho
item.description: Kericho residents were shaken on Sunday, May 17, 2026, after a Climax Coach PSV bus overturned along the Kericho-Nakuru highway at Chepsir. Eyewitnesses and emergency teams quickly responded to the sc...

item.title: Acting Attorney General Discusses Investigations Into 2020 Election Allegations Without Presenting Evidence
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche stated in a recent Fox News interview that there is a substantial amount of evidence suggesting the 2020 presidential election was rigged against former Presi...

item.title: How Pastor Joby Martin Founded The Church of Eleven22 and Built a Growing Congregation
item.description: Pastor Joby Martin, born in South Carolina in 1973, is the founder and lead pastor of The Church of Eleven22 in Jacksonville, Florida. Growing up in a Christian family that only occasionally went to c...

item.title: Cape Town tightens traffic by-laws to allow vehicle impoundment for street racing and reckless driving
item.description: The City of Cape Town is set to introduce stricter traffic by-law amendments aimed at improving road safety and cracking down on dangerous driving behaviours. These changes, recently approved by the S...

item.title: PNP Investigates Cocaine Seizure Worth P34.5 Million in Quezon Town
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has officially launched an investigation after a resident in Patnanungan, Quezon surrendered five tape-sealed bags containing cocaine. The total weight of the seiz...

item.title: Iraqi National Arrested for Planning Attacks on Jewish Sites in the U.S.
item.description: Federal authorities in the United States have arrested Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, a 32-year-old Iraqi national, for allegedly plotting terrorist attacks against Jewish institutions in Los An...

item.title: James Gray's 'Paper Tiger' Receives Strong Reception at Cannes
item.description: At the Cannes Film Festival, James Gray's crime drama 'Paper Tiger' stood out amid a series of underwhelming screenings, bringing energy and excitement to the festival. Starring Adam Driver, Scarlett ...

item.title: Current State of UK-EU Relations and the Implications of a Potential Rejoining Bid
item.description: Labour leadership contenders Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham have reignited debate over the UK's relationship with the EU. Streeting, recently resigned as health secretary, described Brexit as a 'catas...

item.title: Ottawa Charge look to bounce back at home in Walter Cup final against Montreal
item.description: The Ottawa Charge face a critical challenge as they return home for Game 3 of the best-of-five Walter Cup final against the Montreal Victoire. Ottawa is currently down 2-0 after narrowly losing both r...

item.title: Victoria Police monitor gatherings of teenage cyclists in Melbourne
item.description: In Melbourne, groups of teenagers on bicycles, sometimes referred to as 'bike swarms,' have recently been drawing the attention of Victoria Police. These gatherings, often informal ride-outs, are beco...

item.title: Canadian Mining Veterans Merge Companies to Create Strong Domestic Mining Firms
item.description: Veteran Canadian mining financiers, often called the 'OGs' of the sector, are actively working to establish new domestic mining champions. Figures such as Pierre Lassonde, Ross Beaty, Norm Keevil, and...

item.title: Cecilia Vega’s Career Journey and Life Outside Journalism
item.description: Cecilia Marcellina Vega is a prominent American journalist, currently serving as a correspondent for 60 Minutes. Born on 7 January 1977 in San Francisco, California, Cecilia is of Mexican and Italian ...

item.title: Daily Feng Shui Guidance by Master Hanz Cua for May 18, 2026
item.description: On May 18, 2026, Master Hanz Cua shares his daily feng shui insights to guide readers in harmonizing their energy and making wise decisions. These predictions provide advice for improving luck, wealth...

item.title: HURIWA urges investigation into alleged N34.53 trillion diversion of Nigerian government revenue
item.description: The Human Rights Writers Association of Nigeria (HURIWA) don demand say government make dem quickly start investigation after World Bank reveal say about N34.53 trillion revenue of Nigeria dey deducte...

item.title: Philippine Military Academy updates curriculum for modern and digital warfare
item.description: The Philippine Military Academy (PMA) is updating its training curriculum to address the challenges of modern and largely invisible warfare. Vice Admiral Caesar Bernard Valencia, PMA superintendent, i...

item.title: Indian worker dies, three others hurt in Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow region
item.description: In a recent escalation of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, one Indian worker lost his life and three others sustained injuries due to Ukrainian drone attacks in the Moscow region of Russia. The Indian Emb...

item.title: DJhotJantex Hospitalised after Completing 50-Hour Sitting Challenge for Rare Leg Condition Awareness
item.description: Kalenjin entertainer and TikTok star DJhotJantex recently completed a daring 50-hour sitting challenge aimed at raising awareness for her rare leg condition and fighting the stigma surrounding it. The...
